description: Machine-readable rules for VH/VL antibody humanization. STRICTLY SEPARATED from VHH logic.
warning: DO NOT mix with VHH humanization config (tier_system_config.json).

cdr_union_ranges.description: Union of Kabat+Chothia+IMGT+Contact definitions. All positions within these ranges are grafted from mouse, never humanized.

step_2_1_cdr_gate.description: Hard filter: human germline CDR lengths must exactly match mouse antibody
step_2_1_cdr_gate.performance_rule: Single batch Anarcii call, cache results to JSON. Never call per-sequence.

step_2_2_golden_pairs.description: Prioritize VH/VL combinations with high frequency in 458-engineered antibody database

step_2_4_fr_identity.description: Framework region sequence identity, calculated after masking Union CDR ranges

framework_selection_protocol.output: Top-3 VH candidates + Top-3 VL candidates for human review

required_metrics.name: SASA
required_metrics.description: Solvent Accessible Surface Area per Vernier residue

required_metrics.name: contact_number
required_metrics.description: Heavy atom contacts within 4.5 Ang (packing density)

required_metrics.name: vh_vl_angle
required_metrics.description: Inter-domain angle between VH and VL main axes

required_metrics.name: vernier_cdr_distance
required_metrics.description: Distance from each Vernier residue to nearest CDR atom

hard_constraints.name: Gly_Pro_rule
hard_constraints.condition: mouse Vernier residue is G or P

hard_constraints.name: Tier1_default_keep
hard_constraints.reason: T1 positions require structural evidence to override

hard_constraints.name: Buried_salt_bridge
hard_constraints.condition: part of buried salt bridge pair

soft_constraints.name: VH_VL_angle_redline
soft_constraints.condition: mouse L1 >= 11 AND selected human framework L1 <= 10
soft_constraints.note: Three interface positions are co-dependent, must be treated together

soft_constraints.name: L1_VL71_binding
soft_constraints.condition: mouse L1 canonical_class != human framework L1 canonical_class
soft_constraints.data_support: VL71 environment determined 55% by L1 length, r=0.55

soft_constraints.name: VH_core_module_coupling
soft_constraints.decision: if mouse != human at VH73/VH78, keep mouse

soft_constraints.name: H2_VH71_correction
soft_constraints.condition: mouse H2 = H2-10 AND human framework H2 = H2-9

soft_constraints.name: VH48_VH67_antagonism
soft_constraints.decision: avoid introducing bulky residues at both positions simultaneously

sequence_assembly_rules: Replace Union CDR ranges with mouse CDR sequences
Apply backmutation list residue by residue
Self-check: exact string match of mouse CDR1/CDR2/CDR3 in final sequence

vernier_packing.fail_low_cavity: < P5 — large to small substitution, structural instability
vernier_packing.fail_high_clash: > P95 — small to large substitution, folding failure

chemical_liabilities.pattern: exposed Met (SASA>50) or Trp in CDR3

must_do: Execute phases in order: 1 -> 2 -> 3 -> 4 -> 5, no skipping
Use pre-computed cache for VH3 numbering in Phase 2.1
Single batch Anarcii call per germline database (not per sequence)
Complete structure modeling before making backmutation decisions
Base backmutation decisions on actual computed SASA and contact numbers
Generate humanization_proposal.json and obtain human confirmation before assembly
CDR self-check: exact string match after assembly
Load packing reference ranges dynamically from vernier_framework_patterns.json
must_not_do: Mix VHH rules, data, or modules with VH/VL workflow
Skip structure modeling and make backmutation decisions empirically
Humanize any residue within Union CDR ranges
Call Anarcii individually per sequence (performance violation)
Use AI-generated amino acids (only mouse or human germline sequences allowed)
